What it is: Modeled likelihood that a passing fish is contacted by a blade/vane.
How it is calculated: Geometric/kinematic model: f(fish length L, blade number, runner speed/rpm, axial flow velocity); often P = function of L and blade passage frequency.
Key pitfalls / reproducibility issues: Assumes fish arrival timing and orientation (often random/perpendicular); ignores active avoidance; very sensitive to assumed orientation.

What it is: Relative impact speed of blade (or structure) on the fish.
How it is calculated: Measured rig velocity (lab) or computed blade/runner velocity at strike radius (field/CFD).
Key pitfalls / reproducibility issues: Lab single-velocity strikes vs a real runner's velocity DISTRIBUTION; relative vs absolute velocity not always stated.

What it is: Design variables that govern injury at a given strike velocity.
How it is calculated: Blade thickness t; fish-length-to-thickness ratio L/t; leading-edge slant angle.
Key pitfalls / reproducibility issues: Effects are strike-location-dependent; 'blunt/slanted is safer' holds only within tested ranges.

What it is: Where on the body and at what angle the strike occurs.
How it is calculated: Controlled placement (lab) or observed injury location/region (field).
Key pitfalls / reproducibility issues: Controlled in lab; only inferred from injuries in the field; taxonomies differ.

What it is: Fraction injured or killed per strike condition.
How it is calculated: Injured-or-dead count / exposed count, by treatment.
Used by (examples): Most live-fish strike + field passage studies
Key pitfalls / reproducibility issues: Immediate vs delayed; injury thresholds for 'mortal' differ; tag/handling and live-vs-euthanized effects.

What it is: Simulation-predicted contact between fish-as-particles and runner.
How it is calculated: Lagrangian particle tracking or discrete-element method through a CFD flow field; count contacts.
Key pitfalls / reproducibility issues: Particles lack fish behaviour, size and orientation dynamics; field validation is scarce.

What it is: Probability of mortality/injury as a function of strike velocity and fish length.
How it is calculated: Logistic regression: P(mortal) ~ strike velocity x length (+ blade thickness).
Key pitfalls / reproducibility issues: Model form and which injuries count as 'mortal' vary; transfer across species is unreliable.

What it is: Integrates blade strike with other routes/mechanisms into an overall survival.
How it is calculated: Route-weighted combination of strike model with spill/bypass survivals (and life-cycle models).
Key pitfalls / reproducibility issues: Combines uncertain sub-models; mechanism-specific signal is lost in the aggregate.
